subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Afterdrop Phenomenon
description: A quick, practical guide to Afterdrop Phenomenon.

hook.text: A hypothermic person is brought inside and warmed. Their temperature drops further. They go into cardiac arrest.

buildup.text: Afterdrop occurs when cold blood from the extremities returns to the core during rewarming.

discovery.text: This cold blood wave can drop core temperature another 2-5°F even after rescue.

twist.text: Rough handling during rescue can trigger lethal heart rhythms in a hypothermic heart.

climax.text: Gentle handling, warming the core first, and horizontal positioning reduce afterdrop risk.

punchline.text: Rescue can kill if done wrong. Warm the core. Move gently. It's real.

quiz.question: What causes afterdrop in hypothermia patients?
options: Cold blood from extremities returning to the core during rewarming
